title: Nova Locutio Message
description: The structured speech-act record agents exchange. Every message is content-addressed (its hash is computed over its canonical serialization minus the hash and signature fields) and signed by its sender. v0.1 — see spec/README.md for evolution policy and what is deliberately deferred.

schema_version.description: Schema version pinned per message — old messages remain readable as the schema evolves.

kind.description: The speech act this message performs. Discriminates the body shape.

hash.description: Content-hash of the canonical serialization of this message with 'hash' and 'signature' fields removed. The message's global identity.

to.description: DID of the intended receiver, or null for broadcast. v0.1 is single-receiver-or-broadcast; multicast deferred.

in_reply_to.description: Loose conversational threading — the prior message this is responding to. Distinct from body-level fields like 'acks' / 'rejects' / 'retracts' which carry semantic action on prior messages, not just threading.

timestamp.description: Optional ISO 8601 timestamp. Advisory only — semantic ordering is via 'in_reply_to' and (later) causal references. Included in the signed canonical form when present.

constraints.description: Optional constraints (capabilities, budget, deadline). Meaningful primarily for 'request', 'propose', and 'delegate'; ignored by other kinds without being an error.

body.description: Body shape is discriminated by 'kind' — see allOf branches and $defs/*_body.

signature.description: Cryptographic signature from 'from' over the canonical serialization of this message with the 'signature' field removed (the 'hash' field is included in what is signed).

speech_act.description:
Closed v0.1 vocabulary of speech acts. Each is distinct in intent (principle 7); adding a kind requires a schema version bump. Semantics:
 - request: sender asks receiver to perform an action; receiver is expected to comply or reject with reason.
 - assert: sender claims something is true, optionally with evidence.
 - query: sender asks for information; answer typically comes back as an assert.
 - propose: sender offers an option the receiver may accept or decline as a normal outcome (refusal is not a failure).
 - commit: sender binds themselves to an action or promise.
 - retract: sender withdraws one of their own prior statements.
 - delegate: sender grants a capability to the receiver.
 - ack: sender confirms receipt or completion.
 - reject: sender refuses to comply with a prior request, proposal, or delegation.

content_address.description: Content-address: prefix + '_' + 64 lowercase hex chars (BLAKE3-256). Shared with function-record.schema.json — the two schemas must keep this definition in sync.

did.description: W3C Decentralized Identifier. v0.1 expects 'did:key:<multibase>' or 'did:nova:<hash>' but the pattern accepts any valid DID method.

signature.description: v0.1: Ed25519 signature, base64-encoded. Format 'ed25519:<base64>'. Multi-algorithm support deferred.

capability.description: Capability token — same format as function-record.schema.json. Must be kept in sync across schemas.

capabilities.description: Capability tokens claimed by sender (in request/propose) or granted (in delegate).

budget_tokens.description: Maximum tokens the sender authorizes for fulfilling this message.

budget_compute_ms.description: Maximum compute time in milliseconds the sender authorizes.

deadline_ms.description: Deadline in milliseconds relative to receipt. Absolute wall-clock deadlines deferred.

action.description: v0.1 closed vocabulary. 'apply' invokes a function. 'store' contributes a record to the commons. 'validate' asks the receiver to verify properties on a record.

target.description: Hash of the artifact being acted on. Required for 'apply' and 'validate'; omitted for 'store' (the artifact is provided in 'payload').

args.description: Arguments for 'apply'. v0.1 uses the same bare-string-for-function-reference convention as function-record examples.

payload.description: Inline artifact for 'store' (a function record, a proof, etc.). v0.2+ may cross-reference the appropriate schema for validation.

claim.description: The claim being made. v0.1: string in surface syntax. v0.2+: structured claim AST.

evidence.description: Optional content-address of a proof certificate or supporting artifact.

pattern.description: Match criteria. All fields optional; multiple fields are conjunctive (AND).

signature_type.description: Type signature to match. v0.1: string in surface syntax. v0.2+: structured type pattern with unification semantics.

intent_tags.description: Intent tags any matching record must include.

effects.description: Effects the matching record must have at most (subset semantics — purer or equal).

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a prior query response.

propose_body.description: Same structural shape as request_body. The difference is semantic: in 'propose' the receiver declining is a normal outcome, not a failure.

commitment.description: What the sender is binding themselves to. v0.1: string in surface syntax. v0.2+: structured commitment AST.

conditions.description: Preconditions that, if violated, release the sender from the commitment. v0.1 stringified.

retracts.description: Hash of the prior message being retracted. Sender of the retraction must be the same agent that sent the retracted message; receivers MUST verify this.

reason.description: Why the message is being retracted. v0.1 free-form.

capability.description: Capability token being granted to the message's receiver.

expires_at.description: Optional expiration. Null means open-ended (rare; usually granted with a bound).

conditions.description: Conditions under which the delegation is valid. v0.1 stringified.

acks.description: Hash of the message being acknowledged.

result.description: For acknowledging a 'request' or 'query', the result of the action or the query response. Any JSON value; the value sub-language is TBD.

rejects.description: Hash of the message being rejected.

code.description: Closed v0.1 categorical reason. Adding a code requires a schema version bump.

reason.description: Optional human/AI-readable expansion of the categorical code.
